steel_arrowtips.name: Steel arrowtips
steel_arrowtips.examine: Pointed caps of steel. A whole semitone sharper than iron.

steel_arrow.name: Steel arrow
steel_arrow.examine: Steel-tipped arrows. They arrive on the downbeat.

reedwood_logs.name: Reedwood logs
reedwood_logs.examine: Echo-stone-aged reedwood from Quillrook. It whistled while you cut it, which felt judgmental.

composite_shortbow_u.name: Composite shortbow (u)
composite_shortbow_u.examine: A reedwood stave laminated over a steel core. Needs a string before it needs an opinion.

composite_shortbow.name: Composite shortbow
composite_shortbow.examine: Reedwood over steel. The cliffs of Quillrook approve of every shot, audibly.

chimewood_logs.name: Chimewood logs
chimewood_logs.examine: Top-grade wood from Quillrook's echo-grove. It rings when you so much as look at it firmly.

chime_longbow_u.name: Chime longbow (u)
chime_longbow_u.examine: An unstrung chimewood stave. It hums the note it intends to fire.

chime_longbow.name: Chime longbow
chime_longbow.examine: Strung with spidersilk across chimewood. Fires every arrow a quarter-tone sharp, on purpose.

dragon_arrowtips.name: Dragon arrowtips
dragon_arrowtips.examine: Rune stock quenched in echo-stone dust until it forgets how to miss.

dragon_arrow.name: Dragon arrow
dragon_arrow.examine: The finest standard arrow money or smithing can produce. The apology arrives by post.

resonant_arrow.name: Resonant arrow
resonant_arrow.examine: Tipped with echo-stone. The wound keeps ringing after the arrow stops.

bronze_rifle.name: Bronze rifle
bronze_rifle.examine: A long bronze barrel from Brace & Bore. Slow to speak, hard to ignore.

iron_rifle.name: Iron rifle
iron_rifle.examine: An iron long-arm. It reaches the back wall of an argument.

steel_rifle.name: Steel rifle
steel_rifle.examine: Steel-barrelled and patient. Pistols talk fast; this one gets the last word.

mithril_rifle.name: Mithril rifle
mithril_rifle.examine: Light enough to hold steady through a whole verse. The chorus is loud.

rune_rifle.name: Rune rifle
rune_rifle.examine: Storm-grey rune steel, a yard of it. Trades the pistol's patter for one well-placed remark.

longshot_round.name: Longshot round
longshot_round.examine: Long-brass rounds milled for rifles. They commit to the journey.

longshot_bullet_casing.name: Longshot bullet casing
longshot_bullet_casing.examine: Longer brass for longer opinions. Smithed from rune stock.

resonant_round.name: Resonant round
resonant_round.examine: Thunder-shale packed with echo-stone grit. The target hears the shot twice; only the first one is the bullet.

quillrook_quiver.name: Quillrook quiver
quillrook_quiver.examine: An echo-stone-ribbed quiver. Sometimes the cliff hands the shot back, good as new.

marksmans_earmuffs.name: Marksman's earmuffs
marksmans_earmuffs.examine: Standard issue on the Tinnitus Range. The ringing stops; the scores improve; the nickname stays.

resonance_gloves.name: Resonance gloves
resonance_gloves.examine: Echo-stone dust worked into the fingertips. You can feel the note the shot will ring before you loose it.

echo_tokens.name: Echo token
echo_tokens.examine: A slate chit stamped with a quaver. Range-Master Cole counts every echo, and these are the receipts.

echo_stone_shard.name: Echo-stone shard
echo_stone_shard.examine: A sliver of the cliff that answers back. It is faintly repeating everything you say.

echo_target_novice.name: Echo target (novice)
echo_target_novice.examine: A straw boss against the echo-stone. The cliff rings even your worst shots back politely.

echo_target_keen.name: Echo target (keen)
echo_target_keen.examine: A banded target on a tighter note. The cliff has started keeping score honestly.

echo_target_master.name: Echo target (master)
echo_target_master.examine: A slate ring barely wider than the arrow. The echo only answers a perfect pitch.

echo_target_perfect.name: Echo target (perfect)
echo_target_perfect.examine: The Choir's old tuning mark. Hit the note and the whole valley applauds — in your own voice.

reedwood_stand.name: Reedwood reeds
reedwood_stand.examine: Echo-stone-aged reedwood. It whistles in the wind, and louder when cut.

chimewood_stand.name: Chimewood
chimewood_stand.examine: A grove tree that holds every note ever struck near it. The axe is a percussion instrument now.

fletch_bench.name: Fletching bench
fletch_bench.examine: Wren's bench: clamps, knives, feathers, and a sign reading NO HUMMING NEAR THE STAVES.

quillrook_fletcher.name: Fletcher Wren
quillrook_fletcher.examine: She splits echo-stone-aged reedwood by ear. Claims a true shaft whistles in thirds.

quillrook_gunsmith.name: Gunsmith Ada Brace
quillrook_gunsmith.examine: Aldgate Gun Guild, Quillrook outpost. Mills the wrongnote-tinged seam into rounds that answer back.

quillrook_range_master.name: Range-Master Cole
quillrook_range_master.examine: Runs the firing line. Scores your shot by the note the cliff rings back. Hears everything twice.

quillrook_quartermaster.name: Quartermaster Sable
quillrook_quartermaster.examine: Keeps the reward ledger. Pays out in gear for tokens, and in silence for free.

echo_hare.name: Echo hare
echo_hare.examine: It zigzags a half-beat after its own footsteps. The cliff finds this hilarious.

range_rat.name: Range rat
range_rat.examine: Grown fat on dropped rounds and lost arrows. Technically part of the ammo economy.

sharedEdits.what: weaponMode() (~lines 148-151) hardcodes id substrings; without this edit every rifle resolves to MELEE (gunBonus ignored, no _round ammo) and chime_longbow resolves to MELEE. Add rifle + longbow branches.

currentAttackMode() mirrors the same hardcoded substrings client-side (~line 54292 offset; search `currentAttackMode`). Apply the identical rifle + longbow branches so the client HUD/attack style agrees with the server.
sharedEdits.snippet:
if (w && (w.id.includes('pistol') || w.id.includes('rifle') || w.id === 'glock_18')) return 'gun';
  if (w && (w.id.includes('shortbow') || w.id.includes('longbow') || w.id === 'shortbow')) return 'ranged';

sharedEdits.what: Register the new intent domain file (side-effect import, next to the existing `import './intent-misc';` line).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './intent-ranged'; // side-effect: registers range-shot / quill-load / quill-rewards domain intents

sharedEdits.what: Load the Quillrook content pack (append to the import list).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './quillrook';            // Quillrook, the Tinnitus Range (ranged/gun training hub)

sharedEdits.what: Quillrook quiver passive (chance to NOT consume ammo) in the combat ammo-consumption block (~line 702, `const suffix = mode === 'ranged' ? '_arrow' : '_round';`). Insert the quiver check between the ammo validation and `ammo.qty -= 1;`.

GATHER_REQS table (search `const GATHER_REQS`): the gather handler dereferences GATHER_REQS[type].skill, so without these two entries cutting a reedwood/chimewood stand throws server-side. Add alongside the existing Woodcutting rows (skillObjs data ships in this fragment).
sharedEdits.snippet:
reedwood_stand: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },
  chimewood_stand: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },

sharedEdits.what: OPTIONAL polish: new object types (echo_target_*, reedwood_stand, chimewood_stand, fletch_bench) render as the default 'humble marker crate'. If desired, add cases to the object-mesh switch and a minimap color (e.g. echo_target_* -> '#d05050', reedwood/chimewood stands -> '#1e4012'). Game is fully functional without this.
sharedEdits.snippet:
else if (o.type.startsWith('echo_target')) col = '#d05050';
else if (o.type === 'reedwood_stand' || o.type === 'chimewood_stand') col = '#1e4012';
